key_facts: retention_in_days = 14 is already set — resource is compliant
terraform_action must be NONE — do NOT generate any Terraform for this finding
ingestion volume is low (0.25 GB/month) — S3 archival not warranted
368 untagged images accumulating — no lifecycle policy set
61.7 GB total; lifecycle reduces cost from $6.17 to ~$0.44/month
aws_ecr_lifecycle_policy must be a separate resource (not inline)
rules: expire untagged images after 1 day, cap tagged images at 30
data_loss_acknowledged = true — existing untagged images will be deleted

decision_summary.rationale: The log group /aws/lambda/user-auth already has a retention period of 14 days, which is compliant with the current configuration.

/aws/lambda/user-auth.technical_explanation: The log group /aws/lambda/user-auth has a retention period of 14 days, which is already compliant with the current configuration. No further action is required.

decision_summary.rationale: The ECR repository backend-service has 368 untagged images and no lifecycle policy, so adding a lifecycle policy will save $5.73/month.

backend-service-ecr.technical_explanation: The ECR repository backend-service has 368 untagged images and no lifecycle policy. Adding a lifecycle policy will save $5.73/month by removing untagged images after 1 day and capping tagged images to 30.

risk_assessment.verification_steps: Verify that the lifecycle policy does not affect critical images and that the repository is not used by other services.

llm_response.group_summary: Two findings were evaluated. The log group /aws/lambda/user-auth is already compliant, while the ECR repository backend-service requires a lifecycle policy to save $5.73/month. The total estimated monthly savings is $5.73 and the total estimated annual savings is $68.76.

nl_quality.details: Key facts and numbers are well-covered with correct figures, but risk_communication is weak — 'data loss' is a bare label rather than a concrete operational risk, and verification_steps lack specificity (no mention of data_loss_acknowledged=true, no actionable commands or checks).
